full_prompt: Design a single chassis main rail for a tandem-axle heavy road-train trailer to be operated under Australian Design Rule (ADR) 42/45 conditions, with nominal gross trailer mass 34 tonnes distributed 18 t on the two rear axles and 16 t on the kingpin, spanning a wheelbase from kingpin to tandem-axle centroid of 8.5 m. Rail cross section: hot-rolled structural steel I-beam profile, overall depth 350 mm x flange width 120 mm x web thickness 8 mm x flange thickness 14 mm, material AS/NZS 3678 Grade 350 plate (Young's modulus 200 GPa, Poisson 0.30, yield 350 MPa, ultimate 450 MPa, density 7850 kg/m^3). The rail is supported by a kingpin bolster at its forward end and by a tandem suspension bolster at the rear. Load cases: (LC1) static payload 16 t kingpin reaction + 18 t tandem reaction with uniformly distributed load along the deck representing 20 t payload; (LC2) ADR dynamic amplification factor 1.5 on LC1 vertical loads representing pothole/corrugated-road encounter; (LC3) kingpin lateral 0.4 g cornering load applied at 1.2 m height above rail centroid; (LC4) fatigue: 1 million cycles of the LC1 vertical load varying between 40% and 110% of static level, representing ~1e6 km service. Pass/fail: under LC1-LC3, maximum von Mises stress must remain below 233 MPa (350 / 1.5 yield factor), maximum mid-span vertical deflection must remain below span/500 = 17 mm, lateral tip at 1.2 m must remain below 20 mm; under LC4, alternating stress range must remain below the BS 7608 Class F weld detail endurance limit of 40 MPa at 1e6 cycles. Global beam buckling (torsion-flexural) at LC2 amplified load must have linear eigenvalue factor >= 3.0. Deliverables: STEP geometry, per-case FEA stress contours, fatigue check, buckling eigenvalue report, and rail mass per metre of length.
short_prompt:
Design one chassis main rail for a tandem-axle Australian heavy road-train trailer, rolled I-beam in structural steel plate (AS/NZS 3678 Grade 350 class), operated per Australian Design Rule (ADR) 42/45 with BS 7608 fatigue classes for the weld details.

Envelope: kingpin-to-tandem wheelbase 8.5 m; gross trailer mass 34 t distributed 16 t on the kingpin and 18 t on the tandem.

Load cases.
- LC1 static payload: 16 t kingpin + 18 t tandem reactions with a 20 t UDL along the deck.
- LC2 ADR dynamic amplification: 1.5 x LC1 verticals.
- LC3 cornering: 0.4 g lateral at 1.2 m above the rail centroid.
- LC4 fatigue: 1 million cycles of the LC1 vertical varying between 40% and 110% of static.

prompt.title: Heavy-vehicle trailer main chassis rail
prompt.brief: Single I-beam chassis rail for a tandem-axle 34 t road-train trailer per AS/NZS and ADR 42/45.

load_cases.name: static_payload
load_cases.application: supports at kingpin and tandem bolsters
